principle: Always show a visible label above every input field — never use placeholder text as a substitute for a label
explanation: X.ux|form_labels: placeholder_disappears_on_input -> user_loses_context_in_complex_forms; label_always_visible; space_saving_alternative: floating_label (moves_above_field_on_focus); NO:placeholder_as_only_label NO:removing_label_to_save_space eg: "input_placeholder='Your first name'_no_label" >> "label='Your first name'_above + placeholder_as_hint_inside"

principle: Mark optional fields not required ones — most forms have more required fields so marking only optional reduces visual clutter and user anxiety
explanation: X.ux|optional_fields: required_fields == majority_in_most_forms; marking_required_with_asterisks -> visual_clutter + user_anxiety; fix: mark_only_optional_fields with_(optional)_label; form_feels_less_daunting; NO:red_asterisk_on_every_required_field NO:marking_both_required_and_optional eg: "First name* Email* Phone*" >> "First name  Email  Phone (optional)"

principle: Use checkboxes for multiple selection, radio buttons or select for single selection, and always stack options vertically
explanation: X.ux|input_type_selection: multiple_options_selectable -> checkbox; single_option_only -> radio_or_select; stack_options: vertical NOT horizontal -> prevents_miss_clicks + easier_to_read; NO:radio_buttons_for_multi_select NO:horizontal_option_lists eg: "[ ] Red  [ ] Blue  [ ] Green  (horizontal)" >> "[ ] Red\n[ ] Blue\n[ ] Green  (vertical)"

principle: Match input field width to expected content length and remove every field that is not strictly necessary
explanation: X.ux|input_width_and_fields: input_width == expected_content_length (zip_code -> short; address -> wide); every_extra_field -> +drop_off_rate; ask: is_this_data_needed_for_core_action?; NO:asking_phone_number_for_card_subscription NO:uniform_full_width_for_all_inputs eg: "phone_field_on_SaaS_signup" >> "remove_phone_keep_email+password_only"

principle: Split forms with more than five fields into steps with a progress indicator and show inline validation in real time
explanation: X.ux|multi_step_form: 5+_fields -> split_into_steps + progress_indicator (steps_or_%); inline_validation: validate_on_blur NOT only_on_submit; success_state_required: never_leave_user_without_completion_confirmation; NO:single_page_10_field_form NO:validation_only_on_submit NO:no_success_state eg: "10_field_form_one_page" >> "step1:personal(3_fields)+step2:payment(3_fields)+step3:confirm"

principle: Never use translucent or low-contrast input fields — form elements must remain legible on any background and lighting condition
explanation: X.ux|form_accessibility: translucent_inputs + gradient_backgrounds -> fail_for_users_with_visual_impairments + bright_environments; form_fields: solid_background + high_contrast_border + legible_text_at_all_times; NO:semi_transparent_input_on_gradient_hero NO:low_contrast_placeholder_text eg: "rgba_input_on_gradient_bg" >> "white_bg_input_1px_solid_border_#999+_on_any_bg"
